subject: globe-fingerprint-tracker.json has duplicate entries for SEC-6.1 and SEC-6.2
summary: During Mode 4 (globe fingerprint hunt) on 2026-04-15, the analyst noticed that monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprint-tracker.json contains duplicate entries for SEC-6.1 and SEC-6.2. Each duplicate has a 'reviewed' entry (completed 2026-04-11 with aggregate-observational-parasitism findings and defense_neutralization data) and a second 'pending' entry that was never populated. This caused a near-miss in this run: the analyst's Mode 4 pass used t.items.findIndex(i=>i.id==='SEC-6.1') which returns the FIRST match (the already-reviewed one) rather than the pending one, and nearly overwrote the prior-run's authoritative findings with a weaker second-pass note. Recovered by pulling the GitHub version of the tracker, restoring the first entry, and writing the second-pass note into the duplicate-pending entry as reviewed. Remediation: decider should deduplicate the two SEC-6.1 entries (keep the 2026-04-11 aggregate-parasitism entry, remove the empty second one) and same for SEC-6.2 (same double-entry pattern, both still pending). Suggested: one-off node script that removes later duplicates when status==='pending' and findings===null; run on tracker and commit. Also: Mode 4 procedure in analyst-mode34-procedures.md could add a 'check for duplicate IDs first' defensive step, so future analysts don't hit the same issue. This is a minor data-quality fix, not a scientific-review issue.
